title: The makers building Africa’s open-source future
dek: Nairobi’s developer communities are creating public digital tools that governments and start-ups can share.
author: Wanjiku Njoroge
body: Inside a shared workspace near Ngong Road, developers are treating public software as civic infrastructure. Their tools help clinics manage queues, counties publish budgets and schools keep learning materials available offline.
Open-source work rarely attracts the same attention as a venture funding round. Yet it can give smaller institutions access to software they could never afford to commission alone.
The community’s next test is maintenance: building funding models that reward the patient work of keeping critical code secure and useful.
